sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: fat is 6.1g. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. fat is basically not the story here.
sections.subtitleText: fat is 6.1g. omega 3 is doing a lot of the work here. fat is basically not the story here.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: carbs is 64.2g. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for grains, the carb quality matters much more than the raw number.
sections.subtitleText: carbs is 64.2g. fibre is doing a lot of the work here. for grains, the carb quality matters much more than the raw number.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: protein is 14.1g. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein helps, but not enough if the carb side is weak.
sections.subtitleText: protein is 14.1g. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. protein helps, but not enough if the carb side is weak.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin e at 2% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin e at 2% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: magnesium at 49% DV, iron at 25% DV, zinc at 18% DV stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.
sections.subtitleText: magnesium at 49% DV, iron at 25% DV, zinc at 18% DV stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first. unusually strong protein case for a grain because designed to expose whether grain proteins rescue too hard. good mineral support because better than many grains. strong healthy-grain reputation because very likely audience debate food.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: the drawbacks next. GI is only decent because not elite on carb behaviour. prep quality matters because can be unpleasant if badly prepared. can be expensive vs staples because practical downside.

sections.subtitleText: the drawbacks next. GI is only decent because not elite on carb behaviour. prep quality matters because can be unpleasant if badly prepared. can be expensive vs staples because practical downside.

closing.summary: Quinoa does have some solid upside, especially unusually strong protein case for a grain and good mineral support. but GI is only decent and prep quality matters keep it from climbing higher. best when you want a staple carb that actually brings something useful with it.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want a staple carb that actually brings something useful with it.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while vitamins are the biggest drag. It lands in B tier.
